_comment: Generated from 'Complete vaginal agenesis_Medco.pdf' and 'Complete vaginal agenesis_PPD.CPD.pdf'.
clinical_indications: Cyclic pelvic or abdominal pain due to vaginal anomalies
Difficulty with tampon insertion or coitus due to vaginal anomalies
Back pain due to marked distension of the vagina
Pain with defecation due to marked distension of the vagina

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings and advice for admission

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes – all vitals, detailed history, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure, planned line of treatment and advise for admission?
Did the clinical presentation and composite examination (pelvic examination) confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?
Was there an evidence of vaginal agenesis indicative of surgery?

common_queries: Clinical examination and/or imaging is not indicative of surgery, or documentation is missing/incomplete.
Detailed Clinical notes lack essential information (vitals, history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, indication for procedure, planned line of treatment, or advice for admission).
Pelvic/Abdominal USG is not uploaded at pre-authorization.
Diagnosis is not confirmed by clinical presentation and composite examination (pelvic examination).
Detailed procedure/operative notes are missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Discharge summary is missing or lacks follow-up advice at claim submission.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Facilities with well-equipped operation theatre, anesthesia and anesthetist availability
clinical_key_pointers: Complete agenesis of the vagina is almost always associated with absence of uterus. There is, however, presence of healthy gonads and fallopian tubes.
The patient is phenotypically female, with normal female karyotype pattern.
The entity is often associated with urinary tract (40%) and skeletal (12%) malformation. This is called Mayer-RokitanskyKüster-Hauser syndrome.
surgical_options: McIndoe-Reed procedure (1938): A space is created digitally between the bladder and the rectum. Split thickness skin graft is used over a mould. This mould is kept in this neovaginal space.
Williams Vulvovaginoplasty (1976): A vaginal pouch is created from skin faps of labia majora in the midline. This is not done these days.
additional_information.patient_counseling: Treatment of such patients needs repeated psychological counseling.
complications: During surgery: Vesicovaginal fistula, Recto-vaginal Fistula, infection and bleeding are the important ones.
